Document authorized devices/systems

Access to information systems is enforced through Microsoft Entra ID authentication and authorization. Group-based assignments, Conditional Access policies, and least-privilege role enforcement ensure only approved users can access organizational systems.

Non-interactive access is performed using service accounts and service principals with explicitly scoped permissions. Managed identities or credentials stored in Azure Key Vault are used, and interactive logon is disabled to prevent misuse.

Device-based access is enforced using Conditional Access policies requiring Intune-compliant devices. Only devices meeting security baselines such as encryption, OS version, and endpoint protection are allowed to access systems.

Document function permissions
Role-Based Access Control restricts system access to only approved transaction types. Azure RBAC and application permissions ensure users cannot execute unauthorized actions.
System functions are restricted using Entra ID roles and Azure RBAC. Administrative and sensitive functions are accessible only to explicitly authorized roles.

Document security requirements

The use of external information systems is restricted through Azure Firewall, Network Security Groups, and Conditional Access policies. Only approved systems, applications, and network paths are permitted.

Connections to external systems are controlled using network security rules, firewall policies, and Conditional Access. Restrictions are enforced based on destination, identity, device state, and risk.

Entra ID authentication policies enforce limits on consecutive invalid logon attempts. Exceeding the threshold triggers automated protection mechanisms.
Accounts are automatically locked based on defined thresholds or risk signals until released by an administrator or delay algorithm.
Lockout duration is enforced through Entra ID policies, preventing authentication attempts until the defined cooldown period expires.

A system use notification banner is displayed before authentication using Windows and Entra ID policy settings.
Users must acknowledge the system use notification before completing logon. Authentication cannot proceed without acceptance.
Users are informed of the date and time of their last successful logon using Entra ID sign-in data.
